mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so a 3-month downtrend in policy rates predicts higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above their unconditional average after months with a negative 3-month change in FEDFUNDS, or if the sign of the effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5%, the market infers tighter future liquidity and higher equity discount rates, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show a lower 20-day SPX return rate than untriggered episodes, or if the trigger yields positive median returns over a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should depress equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if a rising UNRATE trend does not predict below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears and turns positive in multiple 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months marks labor-market deterioration that should immediately reprice risk assets downward, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the 5-day SPX return following the unemployment-rise trigger is not materially below the unconditional 5-day average, or if the trigger produces positive returns in most occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumption and housing demand, which should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal from RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or if periods of joint strength are followed by negative median returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects stronger industrial output and current-cycle earnings momentum, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in a sustained manufacturing expansion.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%, real rates stay too low and policy is behind the curve; that combination should be associated with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-over-policy lag condition does not predict lower 5-day SPX returns than matched non-trigger periods, or if the interaction term is insignificant across repeated windows.

mechanism_narrative: In the three months after a GDP growth acceleration, equities should reprice higher because stronger aggregate demand raises revenue expectations; the signal is strongest when GDPC1 and RSAFS both improve together.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive on a 2-quarter annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration episodes do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if joint GDPC1/RSAFS improvement fails to outperform the single-series versions in walk-forward testing.
